summary.intent: Implement Telegram forum topic (thread) isolation so that each topic in a supergroup gets its own session, interrupt scope, and reply routing rather than sharing a single session across all topics in a group.
summary.outcome: Full implementation shipped: thread_id added to ChannelReplyTarget, session keys, and DB schema; all Telegram inbound/outbound paths updated to extract and apply message_thread_id; migration created; 696 tests passing; committed, pushed, and PR created.

repo: Session keys follow the format channel_type:account_id:chat_id — thread isolation required appending :thread_id to make it channel_type:account_id:chat_id:thread_id
ChannelReplyTarget is used across all channel crates (Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, MS Teams, Telegram, httpd, web) — any struct field addition requires updates in all of them
SQLite cannot ALTER TABLE to modify a PRIMARY KEY — table recreation via migration is required; the sessions crate uses sqlx migrations in crates/sessions/migrations/
teloxide's Message type exposes thread_id: Option<ThreadId> and is_topic_message: bool; ThreadId wraps MessageId which has an i32 inner field

teloxide's SendMessage (and all other send payloads) support .message_thread_id() setter via SendMessageSetters trait — photo/document/voice/audio each have their own setter trait that must be imported separately
Callback queries provide thread_id via query.message (MaybeInaccessibleMessage) using .regular_message() accessor
The beads issue tracker requires a dolt database initialized from the main repo — it cannot be used from a worktree without the dolt DB present
The just lint recipe uses --all-features which fails on macOS due to CUDA dependency in the providers crate; local-validate.sh uses --all-targets without --all-features for macOS

code.finding: ChannelReplyTarget struct — added thread_id: Option<String> with #[serde(default)] for backward compat, plus outbound_to() method encoding 'chat_id:thread_id' composite string
default_channel_session_key() builds session keys — thread_id appended when present to achieve per-topic isolation
get_active_session/set_active_session signatures updated to accept thread_id: Option<&str>; runtime CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS defines new schema for fresh DBs

Added extract_thread_id(msg) helper and msg_outbound_to(msg) helper; keyboard/card functions (send_sessions_keyboard, send_agent_keyboard, send_context_card, send_model_keyboard, send_sandbox_keyboard) updated to parse composite 'to' and apply message_thread_id
Added parse_chat_target() that splits 'chat_id:thread_id' composite strings; all send methods extract thread_id and apply it to API calls
New migration recreates channel_sessions table to add thread_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '' to the PRIMARY KEY

workflow: Using cargo build to discover all compilation errors across a large multi-crate workspace is more efficient than manually hunting every construction site before compiling
replace_all edits can have unintended reach across different scopes in the same file — verify by checking remaining occurrences after a bulk replace
When a struct field is added with #[serde(default)], backward-compatible deserialization is automatic — no migration needed for JSON-serialized data already in storage

friction: beads issue tracker was unusable from the worktree — dolt DB not initialized there, requiring several failed init attempts before abandoning and proceeding directly with implementation

replace_all edits in handlers.rs accidentally replaced outbound_to() calls in different scopes (main handler vs callback handler), causing use-of-undeclared-variable errors that required follow-up fixes
ChannelReplyTarget construction sites were spread across 11+ files with no single grep pattern that matched all of them — required multiple grep attempts and cargo build to find the last stragglers
The GitHub Discussions API requires GraphQL — the REST API doesn't support posting replies, requiring an extra step to look up the discussion node ID first
open_items: OTP flow bot.send_message() calls in the callback handler use raw ChatId and don't route to the forum topic — intentionally deferred as lower priority
The just lint recipe is broken on macOS with --all-features due to CUDA; pre-existing issue unrelated to this change but worth fixing in the justfile

Reply to the original GitHub discussion (#486) was posted to inform users that Node.js is already included — the Docker image question was resolved, but the Docker-in-Docker / Fly.io sandboxing question from that thread was not addressed
